mission.title: milvus: Support specified shard number when create collection.
mission.description: Support specified shard number when create collection.. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current milvus setup
steps.description:
Verify your milvus version and configuration:
```bash
pip show pymilvus | grep Version
```
This feature requires a working milvus installation.

steps.title: Review milvus configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant milvus configuration:
#### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe:
currently Milvus didn't support user specified shard nums, which brings a  lot of confusing on why the cluster is not balanced.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support specified shard number when create collection.
steps.description:
The number of Partitions and the number of Shards should not exceed 4096.

The increase in QueryNode memory consumption has nothing to do with the number of segments, but with the amount of data to be loaded.

The increase in DataNode memory consumption is indeed related to the number of segments. The reason is that we set the number of growing segment rows to be greater than 32,000 rows before being written to storage. When a large number of segments do not meet this requirement, the memory of

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Support specified shard number when create collection." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
The number of Partitions and the number of Shards should not exceed 4096.

The increase in QueryNode memory consumption has nothing to do with the number of segments, but with the amount of data to be loaded.

The increase in DataNode memory consumption is indeed related to the number of segments.

prerequisites.description: A working milvus installation or development environment.
